commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer compatibility/product judgment, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain issue was found; the diff adds no dependencies/workflows/permissions and uses serialized overlay data plus textContent. (sha=3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends browser `--labels` screenshots to full-page/ref/element Playwright captures, adds optional per-ref `annotations` bounding boxes, updates browser docs/skill guidance, and adds annotation tests.

PR surface: Source +367, Tests +499, Docs +9. Total +875 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows the labels path accepts `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element` at the route but does not forward them to the viewport-only Playwright labels helper.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is maintainer compatibility/product judgment, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser '--labels' screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array. - '--labels --full-page' now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - '--labels --ref ' and '--labels --element ' now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new 'annotations' array of per-ref bounding boxes ({ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. Implementation: - New screenshot-annotate.ts pure helper: refToNumber, planAnnotations with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), buildOverlayInjectionScript and buildOverlayClearScript IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright rewritten to consume the helper, with off-viewport refs no longer filtered (drawn in document space and clipped by the screenshot capture instead). - /screenshot Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - /snapshot Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior changes (documented in docs/cli/browser.md and SKILL.md): - labelsSkipped no longer counts off-viewport refs (only maxLabels truncation and per-ref bbox failures count). - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the annotations field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - screenshot-annotate.test.ts: 24 cases covering refToNumber forms, three coordinate-space projections, maxLabels truncation, empty-name omission, and IIFE script content/idempotency. - pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts: 7 mock-based cases covering all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-not-filtered, and bboxFailures accounting. - All existing 1192 extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Routes that pre-empt to `captureScreenshot()` still do not produce labels (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? A long page (`https://playwright.dev/`, 4121px tall) labeled with `--labels --full-page` returns a JPEG that has annotation boxes drawn at `y` values up to 4121, an `annotations` array of 64 entries with `box.y` matching the same range, and `labelsSkipped: 0`. After the capture, `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. - The semantic narrowing of `labelsSkipped` (no longer counts off-viewport refs). Documented in the Behavior changes section above and in docs/cli/browser.md. - Whether the `/snapshot` Playwright branch should be left out of fullPage/ref/element pass-through. I argue yes; the snapshot command is intentionally a "snapshot text + viewport screenshot" surface. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. I am happy to close the PR if a maintainer wants the work to land via a different seam (e.g. a separate `--annotate` flag, or held until raw-CDP/chrome-mcp parity is in scope). The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` was producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping `ref`). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium 1223 (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b` (the current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after the latest force-push) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser start oc browser navigate https://playwright.dev/ oc browser focus # Case 1: snapshot with annotations oc browser --json snapshot --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot --labels --ref e3 # Case 5: DOM cleanup check oc browser evaluate --fn "() => document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length" ``` - Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Three live screenshots from `https://playwright.dev/`: **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`) — labels visible only in the captured viewport region: **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — 4121px-tall capture; labels span the entire scrollable document, including content far below the fold: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --ref e3`) — capture is clipped to the element rect; labels are projected relative to the element top-left: Sample response (full-page mode at HEAD `3731eb15eb`, copied live; saved image is `401x2000` so coordinates are auto-rescaled by `scaleAnnotations()`): ```json { "ok": true, "path": "/path/to/screenshot.jpg", "url": "https://playwright.dev/", "labels": true, "labelsCount": 64, "labelsSkipped": 0, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 358, "height": 28 } } ] } ``` Sample response (element-clip with `--ref e3`), copied live: ```json { "labelsCount": 4, "annotations": [ { "ref": "e3", "number": 3, "role": "navigation", "name": "Main", "box": { "x": 0, "y": 0, "width": 765, "height": 60 } }, { "ref": "e4", "number": 4, "role": "button", "name": "Toggle navigation bar", "box": { "x": 16, "y": 15, "width": 30, "height": 30 } } ] } ``` - Observed result after fix: | # | Verification | Observed at HEAD `3731eb15eb` | |---|---|---| | 1 | snapshot --labels --json | 64 annotations with `{ref, number, role, name, box}`; numbers correctly parsed from refs (`e3 → 3`) | | 2 | screenshot --labels --json (viewport) | Saved PNG `780×437`; 64 annotations; `labelsSkipped: 0` confirms semantic narrowing. By design, off-viewport refs keep their viewport-relative `box.y` (can exceed image height) so the agent can request a scroll/full-page re-capture instead of seeing them silently dropped. | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page --json | Saved JPEG `401×2000` (resized from original `780×4121` by `normalizeBrowserScreenshot`); 64 annotations rescaled accordingly; `box.y` range `0..1928`, all within image height; **`max(box.y)=1928 ≤ image.height=2000` confirms the rescale fix** (was `0..4121` before the rescale was added). | | 4 | screenshot --labels --ref e3 --json | Saved element-clip PNG `765×60`; 4 annotations all in element-relative coordinates (element itself at `x=0, y=0`); `width=765` and `height=60` match the saved image. | | 5 | DOM cleanup query | `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length` returns `0`; no overlay residue. | - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms it is bypassed (the labels branch checks `usesChromeMcpProfile(profile.driver)` first and routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels`). The `annotations` field is intentionally absent in that branch's response. - Raw-CDP fallback (`captureScreenshot()` when Playwright is unavailable). Code review confirms the `--labels` path routes through Playwright; the fallback is unchanged. - macOS / Windows. Linux only. The change does not touch any OS-specific code paths. - Real `existing-session` regression. I do not have a Chrome 144+ remote debugging session set up locally; the touched route file leaves the chrome-mcp branch byte-identical at the point of the labels call, so I do not believe regression is possible there. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. I do not have a desktop environment in this VM. Headed-mode behavior should be identical because the screenshot path is the same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` call, but I did not verify visually in headed mode. - The dev profile uses an internal-only model provider for the LLM-facing case (verified by reading `safeDetails` propagation in code, not by full agent loop). - Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this fix on the same page and same gateway, `screenshot --labels --full-page` produced a 768x720 PNG with labels visible only in the top viewport; the rest of the document (below the fold) had no labels and no boxes. `screenshot --labels --ref e3` produced an identical viewport PNG, ignoring the ref. The response did not contain an `annotations` field. Reproducible by checking out the parent commit (`aa75477533`) and re-running steps 3 and 4 above. - Live terminal capture (HEAD `3731eb15eb`): This was captured immediately before the PR body was updated, by re-running the L2 evidence harness against the current HEAD. The key invariant is `max(box.y) <= image.height`, which is the exact contract ClawSweeper asked for ("annotation coordinates match the saved image after normalization"). ``` L2 EVIDENCE — HEAD = 3731eb15eb3b545b6211b27f0b28f36905bd938b === [Case 1] viewport: screenshot --labels === saved image: 780 x 437 (PNG) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..4121.0625 note: viewport mode keeps off-viewport refs at viewport coords by design === [Case 2] full-page: screenshot --labels --full-page === saved image: 401 x 2000 (JPEG; resized from original 780 x 4121 by normalizeBrowserScreenshot) labelsCount: 64, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 64 entries, box.y range: 0..1928 check: max(box.y)=1928 <= image.height=2000: True sample annotation: {'ref': 'e3', 'number': 3, 'role': 'navigation', 'name': 'Main', 'box': {'x': 0, 'y': 0, 'width': 358, 'height': 28}} === [Case 3] element-clip: screenshot --labels --ref e3 === saved image: 765 x 60 (PNG) labelsCount: 4, labelsSkipped: 0 annotations: 4 entries, all in element-relative coords: ref=e3 role=navigation box={x: 0, y: 0, width: 765, height: 60} ref=e4 role=button box={x: 16, y: 15, width: 30, height: 30} ref=e5 role=link box={x: 54, y: 14, width: 136.59375, height: 32} ref=e6 role=button box={x: 593.640625, y: 12, width: 155.359375, height: 36} === [Case 4] DOM cleanup === document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length → 0 ``` ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm test extensions/browser pnpm check:changed ``` All green. `pnpm test extensions/browser` reports `1285 passed | 1 skipped (1286)` at HEAD `3731eb15eb`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (24 cases). Pure helper unit tests with no mocks. Covers `refToNumber` for `e`, `ax`, bare numeric, and non-numeric refs; viewport / fullpage / element coordinate projection; element-mode overlap filter; `maxLabels` truncation with `skipped` accounting; empty-name omission; IIFE injection-script content (data-openclaw-labels attr, root id, item coordinates); IIFE clear-script idempotency; empty-input safety. - New `extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases). Mock-based via `pw-tools-core.test-harness.ts`. Covers viewport mode (no fullPage forwarded; annotation projection correct), fullpage mode (`fullPage: true` forwarded; doc-space `box.y` retained), element/ref mode (`refLocator(...).screenshot()` called; annotations projected relative to element rect), `finally`-cleanup-on-error (clear script runs even when screenshot throws), off-viewport refs not filtered, and `bboxFailures` accounting (refs whose `boundingBox()` returns null contribute to `skipped`). What failed before this fix, if known? No automated test failed on `main`. The behavior gaps were silent-drop bugs: parameters were accepted by the route schema and passed through several layers before being dropped at the inner Playwright function call site without any check. CI did not flag them because there was no test asserting that `--full-page --labels` actually produced a full-page output, and no test asserting `--ref --labels` actually produced an element clip. If no test was added, why not? N/A. Tests added for every new code path. I did not add a route-level integration test covering Playwright-path labels on a non-existing-session profile. The existing route test file `agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts` is structured around `requirePwAi: vi.fn(async () => null)`, intentionally exercising the no-Playwright fallback. Adding a parallel Playwright-path harness for marginal coverage felt low-ROI given that the helper layer (24 cases) plus the integration layer (7 cases) plus the live behavior proof above already lock the relevant paths. Happy to add it if a maintainer prefers. ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes. Three documented behavior changes: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Any caller that relied on the silent drop would now see additional labels; I do not believe any caller relies on silently-broken output. - `--labels --ref e3` and `--labels --element ` now produce element-clip output. Previously produced viewport-only output silently. Same compatibility argument. - `labelsSkipped` no longer counts off-viewport refs. The number can only decrease for the same input. Callers that read `labelsSkipped` to detect "too many refs" still work; callers that treat it as "refs not visible in the screenshot" need to switch to filtering `annotations` by image bounds, but I am not aware of such a caller. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions. No changes to SSRF policy enforcement (the existing `assertBrowserNavigationResultAllowed` and `withBrowserNavigationPolicy` calls are unchanged). No new network destinations. No changes to the secret-handling surface. No tool execution changes; `evaluate` and `act` paths are untouched. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations`. The `box` field MUST be in the captured image's coordinate space (viewport-relative for viewport mode, document-relative for fullpage mode, element-relative for element mode). A bug here means agents click on the wrong screen pixel. How is that risk mitigated? - Three coordinate-space projection tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock the math. - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` asserts `fullPage: true` is forwarded and `box.y` is in doc-space (combined assertion at the integration boundary). - Live proof on a 4121px page shows `box.y` values reaching 4121 in fullpage mode but capped at viewport in viewport mode, behaving correctly under both modes. - Element-clip live proof shows the captured element appears at `box: { x: 0, y: 0 }`, confirming the element-relative projection. Secondary risk: DOM residue if cleanup fails. Mitigated by the unconditional `finally { ... clearScript ... }` block in `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright`, the dedicated cleanup-on-error test, and the live `[data-openclaw-labels].length === 0` proof. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review. What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof? Nothing on author. Awaiting CI to settle and a maintainer's call on: - Whether the chrome-mcp / raw-CDP downgrade is acceptable as a follow-up, vs. blocking on parity here. - Whether you would prefer a route-level Playwright-path integration test added before merge. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? None yet (initial PR).
